title: The New PM, Claire Vo | Compile 26
summary: Claire Vo, founder of ChatPRD, discusses how product management is changing in a world where code generation makes building technically easy. She argues that the constraint has shifted from engineering feasibility to finding genuine market demand and novel ideas worth building.
writeup: In this Compile 26 talk, Claire Vo, founder of ChatPRD, examines how product management shifts when code generation makes building technically easy. Her core argument: the binding constraint moves from engineering feasibility to finding genuine market demand and novel ideas worth building. The takeaway is that, as the cost of implementation falls, a product manager's value concentrates in identifying real demand rather than coordinating delivery.
